javax.media.mscontrol.join
Interface Joinable

All Known Subinterfaces:
JoinableContainer, JoinableDialog, JoinableStream, MediaGroup, MediaMixer, MixerAdapter, NetworkConnection, VxmlDialog
public interface Joinable

A Joinable is suitable for media composition.
A Joinable object is either a NetworkConnection, a MediaGroup, a VxmlDialog, or a MediaMixer (base of a Conference application).
It can also be a Stream (audio, video, ...) of any of those objects.
Joinables can be joined/unjoined, to make the media stream flow between them. Join operations can be limited both to a stream (i.e audio,video, see JoinableStream.StreamType) and direction (i.e. SEND, RECV, DUPLEX, see Joinable.Direction).
The join direction can be changed by calling join again, with a different Direction.

Nested Class Summary
static class Joinable.Direction
          Indicates a direction for the media flow.
 
Method Summary
 Joinable[] getJoinees()
           
 Joinable[] getJoinees(Joinable.Direction direction)
           
 void join(Joinable.Direction direction, Joinable other)
          Establish a media stream between this object and other.
 void joinInitiate(Joinable.Direction direction, Joinable other, java.io.Serializable context)
          Initiates the same action as join(javax.media.mscontrol.join.Joinable.Direction, Joinable), but returns immediately, without waiting for the join completion (asynchronous operation).
 void unjoin(Joinable other)
          Disconnect any media streams flowing between this object and other's.
 void unjoinInitiate(Joinable other, java.io.Serializable context)
          Asynchronous version of unjoin.
 

Method Detail

getJoinees

Joinable[] getJoinees(Joinable.Direction direction)
                      throws MsControlException
Parameters:
direction - filter on the connection direction
Returns:
an array of other Joinables to which we are joined, exclusive/restrictive list: Only the Joinables with at least the same streams are listed. (e.g. if this Joinable has both audio and video stream, only Joinables connected both with audio and video are listed).
If direction is SEND, then all objects joined in SEND or DUPLEX are included.
If direction is RECV, then only the objects joined in RECV or DUPLEX are included.
If direction is DUPLEX, then only the objects joined in DUPLEX are included.
The result includes only the Joinables directly connected to us (does not jump across MediaMixers).
Throws:
MsControlException

getJoinees

Joinable[] getJoinees()
                      throws MsControlException
Returns:
Other Joinables to which we are connected, on at least one stream (possibly more), and at least in one direction (Inclusive list).
The result includes only the Joinables directly connected to us (does not jump across MediaMixers).
Throws:
MsControlException

join

void join(Joinable.Direction direction,
          Joinable other)
          throws MsControlException
Establish a media stream between this object and other.
The Direction argument indicates a direction; The order of the arguments helps to remember which is the origin and which is the destination. For example:
objectA.join(Direction.SEND, objectB);
means that
objectA sends to objectB
The result is strictly equivalent to objectB.join(Direction.RECV, objectA).

If objectA is a MediaMixer (implementing a conference) and objectB is a NetworkConnection (representing a conference participant), this means that the participant is "muted".

Joining again the same pair of objects ("re-joining")

The given direction replaces a possibly existing relationship between the objects.
For example:
ObjectA.join(REVC, ObjectB)
followed by
ObjectA.join(SEND, ObjectB)
results in ObjectA sending to ObjectB (not duplex, the SEND direction is not "added" to the RECV direction).

Joining an object to multiple other objects

The application is allowed to join objectA to objectB, objectC, etc. The resulting relationship is:
  • objectA will send data to all others (broadcast to objectB, objectC, etc.)
  • objectA will only receive from the last joined object
For example:
ObjectA.join(DUPLEX, ObjectB)
ObjectA.join(DUPLEX, ObjectC)
ObjectA.join(DUPLEX, ObjectD)
ObjectA sends the same stream(s) to ObjectB, ObjectC and ObjectD.
ObjectA only receives from ObjectD

Joining across MediaSessions

It is allowed to join MediaObjects created by the same MediaSession, or created by MediaSessions of the same MsControlFactory.
Joining objects relating to different MsControlFactories (of the same or different Drivers) is optionally supported.

Parameters:
direction - indicates direction (DUPLEX, SEND, RECV)
other - Joinable object to connect
Throws:
MsControlException
java.lang.IllegalStateException - if the object has been released
TooManyJoineesException - if the number of joined objects is too high (value is implementation dependent)
TranscodingException - if the media server is unable to perform the transcoding required by the join

unjoin

void unjoin(Joinable other)
            throws MsControlException
Disconnect any media streams flowing between this object and other's.

Note: Changing the direction (e.g. changing from DUPLEX to RECV), is obtained by calling join again, with the desired direction.

Parameters:
other - Joinable object to disconnect
Throws:
MsControlException
java.lang.IllegalStateException - if the object has been released

joinInitiate

void joinInitiate(Joinable.Direction direction,
                  Joinable other,
                  java.io.Serializable context)
                  throws MsControlException
Initiates the same action as join(javax.media.mscontrol.join.Joinable.Direction, Joinable), but returns immediately, without waiting for the join completion (asynchronous operation). The application is later notified of the completion by a JoinEvent sent to the JoinableContainer's listener, or the JoinableDialog's listener.
In the case of a JoinableStream, the listener of the parent JoinableContainer is called.

Parameters:
direction - see the join method
other - see the join method
context - an opaque object, that will be provided in the completion event.
Throws:
MsControlException
java.lang.IllegalStateException - if the object has been released

unjoinInitiate

void unjoinInitiate(Joinable other,
                    java.io.Serializable context)
                    throws MsControlException
Asynchronous version of unjoin.

See unjoin(Joinable) and joinInitiate(javax.media.mscontrol.join.Joinable.Direction, Joinable, Serializable)

Throws:
MsControlException
